key_insight: This survey frames large language models as three complementary roles in vehicle routing: translating requirements into models, generating routing strategies, and coordinating tools or agents.
engineering_relevance: It is relevant to logistics and industrial automation because it suggests a natural-language interface for building and solving complex routing systems with operational constraints.
method: Classifies LLM use in VRP into modelers, designers, and coordinators.
Summarizes VRP variants, solver families, benchmarks, and evaluation frameworks.
Reviews two comparative experiments and connects LLMs with external tools and neural solvers.
evidence_signal: The paper is a survey that synthesizes existing studies and reports benchmarks, real or near-real datasets, evaluation frameworks, and two comparative experiments.
watch_item: LLM outputs must still be validated carefully, since routing problems are constraint-heavy and incorrect model translation or tool orchestration can produce infeasible plans.
paper_title: Vehicle Routing Problem Meets Large Language Models: An Overview and Perspectives
